tossups.question: The orchestra is split into five groups on different parts of the stage in this composer’s Archipelago S. This composer’s second collaboration with one director, after Dodes’ka-den, included the track “Hell’s Picture Scroll” in a battle scene; that movie is Akira Kurosawa’s (*) Ran. This composer secluded himself in a villa with Debussy scores in order to write “November Steps”. Stravinsky took this composer out to lunch after hearing his Requiem for Strings. For ten points, name this Messiaen-inspired classical Japanese composer.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Toru Takemitsu (accept names in either order)
tossups.question_sanitized: The orchestra is split into five groups on different parts of the stage in this composer's Archipelago S. This composer's second collaboration with one director, after Dodes'ka-den, included the track "Hell's Picture Scroll" in a battle scene; that movie is Akira Kurosawa's (*) Ran. This composer secluded himself in a villa with Debussy scores in order to write "November Steps". Stravinsky took this composer out to lunch after hearing his Requiem for Strings. For ten points, name this Messiaen-inspired classical Japanese composer.

tossups.question: This composer combined recordings of tap dancers with the diegetic sound of the chips clacking during a gambling game played by gangsters in a film whose climactic murder is set to “Dido’s Lament.” A 1962 film begins with atonal music by this composer playing over shots of mist swirling around an empty suit of armor. His use of silence is exemplified by a scene where the music abruptly stops when a gunshot rings out during the attack on the third (*) castle in a 1985 film. With a director he often collaborated with, this composer scored a documentary about Antoni Gaudí (“gow-DEE”) and used string glissandi to represent shifting sands in Woman in the Dunes. He incorporated the biwa into his score for Harakiri. For 10 points, name this composer who scored Ran and many Japanese New Wave films.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Toru Takemitsu (The first film is Pale Flower.)
tossups.question_sanitized: This composer combined recordings of tap dancers with the diegetic sound of the chips clacking during a gambling game played by gangsters in a film whose climactic murder is set to "Dido's Lament." A 1962 film begins with atonal music by this composer playing over shots of mist swirling around an empty suit of armor. His use of silence is exemplified by a scene where the music abruptly stops when a gunshot rings out during the attack on the third (*) castle in a 1985 film. With a director he often collaborated with, this composer scored a documentary about Antoni Gaudi ("gow-DEE") and used string glissandi to represent shifting sands in Woman in the Dunes. He incorporated the biwa into his score for Harakiri. For 10 points, name this composer who scored Ran and many Japanese New Wave films.

tossups.question: John Williams believed that a folk instrument from this country sounded “like a dinosaur’s cry,” which led him to include it in the score for Jurassic Park. The “1.8” variety of that instrument from this country has a fundamental of D4 and is tuned to the minor pentatonic scale. Musical instruments from this country were featured in a piece the New York Philharmonic commissioned for its 125th anniversary, November Steps. A traditional flute from this country is used in the practice of “blowing meditation” practiced by the fuke sect of monks. This country’s national instrument, a typically 13-stringed zither called a koto, is often included in gagaku orchestras. For 10 points, name this country whose traditional instruments include the taiko drum and shakuhachi flute.
tossups.answer: Japan [or Nihon-koku or Nippon-koku] (November Steps is by Tōru Takemitsu.)

packet.name: M - Liberty B, Northwestern A, NYU A, Warwick A, Waterloo A

tossups.question: A “schizo-eclectic” piece from this country is subtitled “Say sea, take me!” in reference to the influence of Debussy’s La mer on the composer. A one-movement piece from this country includes several long pauses during which poetry by Rabindranath Tagore is to be recited. After hearing that piece from this country, Eclipse, Leonard Bernstein made suggestions that inspired its composer to write the piece (*) November Steps. A pianist born in this country won the 1989 Instrumental Gramophone Award for her recording of all eighteen Mozart piano sonatas. The book Absolutely on Music details “conversations” with a conductor from this country who was known for wearing a signature white turtleneck during his 29 year tenure as conductor of the Boston Symphony Orchestra. For 10 points, name this birthplace of Tōru Takemitsu, Mitsuko Uchida, and Seiji Ozawa.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Japan [accept Nihon-koku or Nippon-koku] (The "schizo-electric" piece is Takemitsu's Quotation of Dream.)
tossups.question_sanitized: A "schizo-eclectic" piece from this country is subtitled "Say sea, take me!" in reference to the influence of Debussy's La mer on the composer. A one-movement piece from this country includes several long pauses during which poetry by Rabindranath Tagore is to be recited. After hearing that piece from this country, Eclipse, Leonard Bernstein made suggestions that inspired its composer to write the piece (*) November Steps. A pianist born in this country won the 1989 Instrumental Gramophone Award for her recording of all eighteen Mozart piano sonatas. The book Absolutely on Music details "conversations" with a conductor from this country who was known for wearing a signature white turtleneck during his 29 year tenure as conductor of the Boston Symphony Orchestra. For 10 points, name this birthplace of Toru Takemitsu, Mitsuko Uchida, and Seiji Ozawa.

tossups.question: One work by this composer contains movements like Study in Vibration and Study in Intonation and contains no traditional musical notation. Greenpeace commissioned a Moby Dick inspired work by this composer that features an S-E-A motif. This composer of Toward the Sea and Corona arranged Barry Mason and Les Reed’s song (+) The Last Waltz for solo guitar. The poetry of Rabindranath Tagore is read above one work by this composer, while this composer’s last piece for solo piano is subtitled “in memoriam Olivier Messiaen” after this composer’s “spiritual mentor”. This composer wrote a viola concerto which takes its title from a poem by (*) Makota Ōoka. This composer included parts for the biwa and shakuhachi, traditional instruments from his home country, in pieces like Eclipse and November Steps. For 10 points, name this modern Japanese composer of works like Rain Tree Sketch and The String Around Autumn.
tossups.question_sanitized: One work by this composer contains movements like Study in Vibration and Study in Intonation and contains no traditional musical notation. This composer wrote a viola concerto which takes its title from a poem by (*) Makota Ooka. This composer included parts for the biwa and shakuhachi, traditional instruments from his home country, in pieces like Eclipse and November Steps. For 10 points, name this modern Japanese composer of works like Rain Tree Sketch and The String Around Autumn.

bonuses.leadin: This composer collected ukiyo-e ("oo-kee-yo-EH") prints and cultivated a Japanese-style garden at his house at La Belvédère. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This composer collected ukiyo-e ("oo-kee-yo-EH") prints and cultivated a Japanese-style garden at his house at La Belvedere. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer who used a gong and stereotypical pentatonic melodies in the movement Laideronette, Empress of the Pagodas from his Mother Goose Suite.

The first published score of this Japonisme-influenced piece featured Hokusai's print The Great Wave off Kanagawa on the cover. Claude Debussy wrote this set of "three symphonic sketches for orchestra."
This composer, who called Debussy his "great mentor," used the musical cryptogram "S - E - A" in his piece Towards the Sea.
parts_sanitized: Name this composer who used a gong and stereotypical pentatonic melodies in the movement Laideronette, Empress of the Pagodas from his Mother Goose Suite.

The first published score of this Japonisme-influenced piece featured Hokusai's print The Great Wave off Kanagawa on the cover. Claude Debussy wrote this set of "three symphonic sketches for orchestra."
This composer, who called Debussy his "great mentor," used the musical cryptogram "S - E - A" in his piece Towards the Sea.
answers: La Mer [La Mer, trois equisses symphoniques pour orchestre; or The Sea; Three Symphonic Sketches for Orchestra]
answers_sanitized: La Mer [La Mer, trois equisses symphoniques pour orchestre; or The Sea; Three Symphonic Sketches for Orchestra]

bonuses.leadin: This composer took scores of Prelude to the Afternoon of a Faun and Jeux to a mountain villa, where he wrote the first section of a piece commissioned for the 125th anniversary of the New York Philharmonic. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this mostly self-taught composer whose Requiem for string orchestra received praise from Igor Stravinsky. This composer's “great mentor” Claude Debussy inspired pieces such as Quotation of Dream.
Rain Tree Sketch II by Takemitsu was written following the death of this composer, his “spiritual mentor.” This French composer notably called for ondes Martenot in his Turangalila Symphony.
Takemitsu’s experimental piece Corona, consisting of five etudes represented in the score as circles, pulls from his time learning from this American composer of Sonatas and Interludes and 4’33’’.
answers_sanitized: Olivier Messiaen [or Olivier Eugene Prosper Charles Messiaen]
John Cage [or John Milton Cage Jr.]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This composer took scores of Prelude to the Afternoon of a Faun and Jeux to a mountain villa, where he wrote the first section of a piece commissioned for the 125th anniversary of the New York Philharmonic. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this mostly self-taught composer whose Requiem for string orchestra received praise from Igor Stravinsky. This composer's "great mentor" Claude Debussy inspired pieces such as Quotation of Dream.
Rain Tree Sketch II by Takemitsu was written following the death of this composer, his "spiritual mentor." This French composer notably called for ondes Martenot in his Turangalila Symphony.
Takemitsu's experimental piece Corona, consisting of five etudes represented in the score as circles, pulls from his time learning from this American composer of Sonatas and Interludes and 4'33''.

bonuses.leadin: These objects both title and inspired Takashi Yoshimatsu’s opus 67 piano concerto. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this type of object that is also the subject of the Japanese folk song “Sakura, Sakura.” The Korean national anthem “Arirang” is inspired by the story of two lovers picking some of these objects.

This Japanese composer likened his music to the stillness of grass and flowers. This composer integrated Western and Japanese classical music styles in his piece November Steps, written for biwa, shakuhachi, and orchestra.
This other Japanese composer included his piece “A Flower is Not A Flower” on his album Playing the Piano. He also composed the albums async and Thousand Knives.
answers_sanitized: flowers [accept cherry blossoms; accept Memo Flora; accept camellias]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: These objects both title and inspired Takashi Yoshimatsu's opus 67 piano concerto. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this type of object that is also the subject of the Japanese folk song "Sakura, Sakura." The Korean national anthem "Arirang" is inspired by the story of two lovers picking some of these objects.

This Japanese composer likened his music to the stillness of grass and flowers. This composer integrated Western and Japanese classical music styles in his piece November Steps, written for biwa, shakuhachi, and orchestra.
This other Japanese composer included his piece "A Flower is Not A Flower" on his album Playing the Piano. He also composed the albums async and Thousand Knives.

bonuses.leadin: Paavo Järvi (PAH-vo YAIR-vee) is the principal conductor of an orchestra in this country that performs in Suntory Hall. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this country, home to the NHK symphony orchestra, which has a tradition of assembling a 10,000-person chorus to sing Beethoven’s Ninth Symphony every New Year.

This Japanese conductor served as the Boston Symphony Orchestra’s musical director for 29 years until 2002, and he later had six conversations with Haruki Murakami published in the book Absolutely On Music.
Ozawa conducted the premiere of this Japanese composer’s November Steps for biwa, shakuhachi, and orchestra. This composer wrote the score to Akira Kurosawa’s Ran (ron).
answers_sanitized: Toru Takemitsu [accept names in either order]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Paavo Jarvi (PAH-vo YAIR-vee) is the principal conductor of an orchestra in this country that performs in Suntory Hall. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this country, home to the NHK symphony orchestra, which has a tradition of assembling a 10,000-person chorus to sing Beethoven's Ninth Symphony every New Year.

This Japanese conductor served as the Boston Symphony Orchestra's musical director for 29 years until 2002, and he later had six conversations with Haruki Murakami published in the book Absolutely On Music.
Ozawa conducted the premiere of this Japanese composer's November Steps for biwa, shakuhachi, and orchestra. This composer wrote the score to Akira Kurosawa's Ran (ron).

packet.name: 09 Minarik et al. + Nguyen et al.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about “aleatoric” music, which incorporates random elements. For 10 points each:
parts: This composer’s In C contains 53 different phrases, which generally must be played in order, but other aspects, such as speed, number of repetitions, or instrumentation can vary between performances.
This John Cage piece titled for a length of time is aleatoric, because the only sound comes from ambient noise that varies across performances.

Groups of orchestral sections repeat short passages until the conductor orders them to move on in the aleatoric piece Arc II: Textures by this composer of Toward the Sea and the viola concerto The String Around Autumn.
answers: 4’33” [“four thirty three;” or “four minutes, thirty three seconds”]
answers_sanitized: 4'33" ["four thirty three;" or "four minutes, thirty three seconds"]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about "aleatoric" music, which incorporates random elements. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: This composer's In C contains 53 different phrases, which generally must be played in order, but other aspects, such as speed, number of repetitions, or instrumentation can vary between performances.
This John Cage piece titled for a length of time is aleatoric, because the only sound comes from ambient noise that varies across performances.

Groups of orchestral sections repeat short passages until the conductor orders them to move on in the aleatoric piece Arc II: Textures by this composer of Toward the Sea and the viola concerto The String Around Autumn.

bonuses.leadin: Name these modern composers whose music has been used in films, for 10 points each:
parts: Excerpts from this composer’s Lux Aeterna can be heard in the moonbus scene from 2001: A Space Odyssey. This composer is best known for micropolyphonic music such as Atmospheres and Apparitions.

This composer received an honorary Academy Award in 2007 for his contributions to film music. This Italian film composer’s most famous piece is probably “The Ecstasy of Gold” from The Good, The Bad and The Ugly.

This composer made careful use of silence in the scores he composed for over 100 films, such as after a gunshot during a battle scene in Ran. He’s better known for pieces like Rain Coming and Rain Tree Sketch II, the latter of which was dedicated to his idol Olivier Messiaen.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Name these modern composers whose music has been used in films, for 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Excerpts from this composer's Lux Aeterna can be heard in the moonbus scene from 2001: A Space Odyssey. This composer is best known for micropolyphonic music such as Atmospheres and Apparitions.

This composer received an honorary Academy Award in 2007 for his contributions to film music. This Italian film composer's most famous piece is probably "The Ecstasy of Gold" from The Good, The Bad and The Ugly.

This composer made careful use of silence in the scores he composed for over 100 films, such as after a gunshot during a battle scene in Ran. He's better known for pieces like Rain Coming and Rain Tree Sketch II, the latter of which was dedicated to his idol Olivier Messiaen.

bonuses.leadin: This composer worked on the film scores for Pitfall and Empire of Passion. For 10 points each.
parts: Name this composer of a Requiem for strings and Rain Tree Sketch, the latter of which uses his E-flat - E - A motive, representing the sea.

The Japanese pianist Mitsuko Uchida is renowned for her performances of Mozart, as well as her recordings of this composer of the Wanderer Fantasy and “Ellen’s Third Song,” which is popularly known as his Ave Maria.

The then 14-year-old Japanese violinist Midori Goto famously broke two strings during a 1986 performance of this American composer’s Serenade for violin. This composer also wrote the music for Candide and West Side Story.

The then 14-year-old Japanese violinist Midori Goto famously broke two strings during a 1986 performance of this American composer's Serenade for violin. This composer also wrote the music for Candide and West Side Story.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about the world of classical guitar, FTPE.
parts: Perhaps the most famous work for guitar and orchestra is the Concierto de Aranjuez by this Spanish composer.

This composer included a prominent guitar part in his To the Edge of Dream. He also did piano pieces like his Rain Tree Sketch dedicated to the memory of Messiaen. Later in life, he developed an interest in the music of his native Japan, which he'd previously shied away from.

This Spanish guitar virtuoso drew inspiration from the drums of Algeria to write his Danza Mora, but his single most famous piece is a solo work inspired by a Moorish palace, entitled Recuerdos de la Alhambra.
